novel_features.name: Ghost Service Detector
novel_features.description: Surface trips that are scheduled in GTFS but absent from live real-time data — the buses that exist on paper but never showed up
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining local GTFS stop_times with live estimated-timetable API responses — no public transit tool does this comparison
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to know whether a scheduled service is actually running today, this is the only command that answers definitively

novel_features.name: Last Viable Departure
novel_features.description: Find the latest departure from a stop that still reaches your destination before a cutoff time
novel_features.rationale: Walks GTFS stop_times backward from the cutoff across transfer points — unavailable in TBM app or any existing tool
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent plans evening activities, this answers 'what is the absolute latest I can leave and still get home by transit'

novel_features.name: Delay-Triggered Reroute
novel_features.description: When your current connection is delayed, compute the best alternate onward path using live vehicle data
novel_features.rationale: Joins real-time vehicle positions with local GTFS graph to find alternatives — reactive mid-journey planning no web tool offers
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent detects a delay notification, this computes the optimal recovery without re-planning from scratch

novel_features.name: Alert Impact Filter
novel_features.description: Filter all active disruptions to only those affecting your specific lines or stops
novel_features.rationale: Cross-references alert line/stop references against a user-supplied watchlist — existing tools show all alerts with no filtering
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent monitors a commuter's daily lines, this filters noise to only relevant disruptions

novel_features.name: Line Stop Sequence
novel_features.description: Print the ordered stop list for a line and direction, optionally with scheduled departure times
novel_features.rationale: Offline-capable ordered stop sequence from local GTFS — pipe-friendly output for printing, scripting, or downstream analysis
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs the exact stop order for a tram line to plan boarding points or give directions, this is the canonical answer

novel_features.name: Timetable Change Diff
novel_features.description: Compare GTFS sync snapshots to detect added, removed, or modified routes on a line
novel_features.rationale: Diffs versioned local GTFS data across sync timestamps — no tool surfaces week-over-week schedule changes
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent manages a commuter's routine, this proactively detects schedule changes that would break their timing

novel_features.name: Journey Planner
novel_features.description: Plan multi-modal journeys across tram, bus, and ferry using local GTFS data with live disruption awareness
novel_features.rationale: Offline-capable connection scan over local SQLite GTFS graph — scriptable, disruption-aware, no dependency on Google Maps
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs transit directions in Bordeaux without relying on third-party map APIs, this plans the route locally

novel_features.name: Headway Frequency Report
novel_features.description: Compute average headways per hour from the SIRI estimated timetable
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates stop_times across calendar patterns in SQLite — reveals when service thins out, invisible in raw timetables
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent evaluates transit reliability for scheduling, this shows the real-world gaps between departures by time of day

narrative.headline: Every Bordeaux tram, bus, and ferry schedule offline in SQLite, plus real-time arrivals, ghost service detection, and journey planning no other Bordeaux transit tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: Syncs the full TBM GTFS dataset locally so schedule lookups, stop searches, and frequency analysis work without an internet connection. Overlays live real-time data from the SIRI-Lite API for arrival predictions, vehicle tracking, and disruption alerts. Computes journeys, detects ghost services, and diffs timetable changes — capabilities no existing Bordeaux transit tool provides.

quickstart.comment: Health check — verifies API reachability and local store state
Download the full GTFS dataset into local SQLite
Tram A headway patterns by hour

troubleshoots.fix: Service may have ended for the day. Check with `infotbm-pp-cli lines frequency --line <line>` to see operating hours.
The GTFS ZIP is ~15MB. Retry with `infotbm-pp-cli sync --full --timeout 60s`.
Use `stops --name <name>` to find the full stop reference (e.g., bordeaux:StopPoint:BP:3648:LOC). Short codes like BEQUI also work for some endpoints.

narrative.when_to_use: Use when an agent needs Bordeaux transit information — schedules, real-time arrivals, route planning, or disruption monitoring. Ideal for commute automation, travel planning, and transit-aware applications in the Bordeaux Métropole area.

recipes.explanation: Check next departures at your stop with only the fields that matter

recipes.explanation: Filter alerts to only tram lines before a weekend outing

recipes.explanation: Find the latest departure that still gets you home

recipes.explanation: Surface scheduled trams that are not showing up in real-time data

recipes.explanation: Detect any trips added, removed, or shifted since last week's sync
